channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Marcos Arrives in Cebu to Lead ASEAN Summit 2026 Discussions
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. arrived in Cebu on Wednesday evening to preside over the 48th ASEAN Summit, hosted by the Philippines as the current chair of the regional organization. He la...

item.title: Pakistan to face severe heatwave from May 7 to 11, PMD warns
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a forecast for extreme heat conditions across various parts of Pakistan from May 7 to 11. The southern and central regions, including cities lik...

item.title: Sindh Police Suspends Three Officers Over Alleged Mistreatment of Aurat March Activists Including Sheema Kermani
item.description: In a notable development, the Sindh police have suspended three officials including DSP Nasir Afridi, SHO Hina Mughal, and SHO Nadeem for the alleged mistreatment of Aurat March activists, among them ...

item.title: How to Get Cheap MTN, Airtel, and Glo Data in 2026
item.description: For 2026, Nigerians don dey find better ways to save on mobile data as internet costs don dey high. MTN, Airtel, and Glo get secret USSD codes wey fit give you better deals, known as 'Awoof' bundles. ...

item.title: Congress Offers Conditional Support to TVK as Vijay Stakes Claim to Form Tamil Nadu Government
item.description: The Congress Party has announced its support for actor-politician Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) to form the government in Tamil Nadu. A Congress delegation handed over a letter of support to ...

item.title: Greece overtakes Spain in UK summer holiday bookings following border changes
item.description: In a notable shift in travel trends, Greece has overtaken mainland Spain in summer holiday bookings from UK travellers. Traditionally, Spain has been the top destination for British holidaymakers, but...

item.title: Canadian Privacy Watchdogs to Release Findings on OpenAI's Data Practices
item.description: Federal and provincial privacy authorities in Canada are set to release a joint report on OpenAI, the company behind the AI chatbot ChatGPT. The investigation, initially launched over three years ago,...

item.title: TIB and Tiseza sign MoU to boost investment and development of Special Economic Zones in Tanzania
item.description: TIB Development Bank has entered into a strategic partnership with the Tanzania Investment and Special Economic Zones Authority (Tiseza) to accelerate the development of Special Economic Zones (SEZs) ...

item.title: Key Outcomes from the First Global Summit on Phasing Out Fossil Fuels
item.description: Almost 60 countries, representing roughly a third of the global economy, gathered in Santa Marta, Colombia, for the first international summit focused on ending fossil fuel reliance. The summit marked...

item.title: Assessing the Impact of China’s Zero-Tariff Deal on African Trade
item.description: China has announced zero-tariff access for imports from 53 African nations, excluding Eswatini, effective from 1 May 2026 for two years. This expansion follows an earlier phase covering 33 African lea...

item.title: Analysis of Seasonal Pollution Trends Essential for Targeted Air Quality Management in Indian Cities
item.description: A decade-long review of air quality in Indian cities, including Delhi, reveals that pollutants such as PM2.5, PM10, NO2, CO, and ozone display distinct seasonal patterns. Research by Envirocatalysts, ...

item.title: Study Warns B.C. of Potential Landslide-Triggered Tsunamis After Massive Alaska Event
item.description: A recent study published in Science has revealed that a landslide in Alaska's Tracy Arm Fjord last year caused one of the largest tsunamis ever recorded, with waves reaching 481 metres, surpassing the...

item.title: Role of Private Schools in Kenya’s Shift to Competency-Based Education
item.description: This opinion piece discusses the evolving role of private schools in Kenya as the country transitions from the traditional 8-4-4 system to Competency-Based Education (CBE). Education is highlighted as...

item.title: University of Ibadan Students Protest Disruption of Campus Services Due to NASU Strike
item.description: Students for University of Ibadan don begin peaceful protest for campus because many basic services don shut down as Non-Academic Staff Union (NASU) dey on strike. Dem dey call on Federal Government m...

item.title: Three individuals, including a 15-year-old, arrested in Lurgan over car bomb inquiry
item.description: Police in Lurgan have arrested three individuals, including a 15-year-old boy, in connection with an investigation into a car bomb incident near the local police station. Authorities have not disclose...

item.title: Marc Lore Plans AI-Driven Platform for Launching Virtual Restaurants
item.description: Marc Lore, a seasoned entrepreneur behind past ventures acquired by Amazon and Walmart, is spearheading a new AI-driven project called Wonder. Central to this initiative is Wonder Create, a platform d...

item.title: Alex Eala wins opening match at Italian Open, advances to second round
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Alex Eala successfully redeemed herself in her return to the Italian Open, winning her first-round match against Poland's Magdalena Frech with a score of 6-0, 3-6, 6-4 at the For...

item.title: Vogtle Nuclear Project: Cost Overruns and Lessons for US Energy Policy
item.description: Vogtle Units 3 and 4 in Waynesboro, Georgia, intended to mark a revival of US nuclear energy, have become a case study in mismanagement and cost overruns. Approved in 2009 at an initial projected cost...

item.title: Allahabad High Court grants anticipatory bail to student in religious conversion case
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has granted anticipatory bail to a Class 12 Muslim student, Malishka Fatma, who faced accusations of attempting to convert her Hindu classmate. The court, led by Justice Avnis...

item.title: Rivers Youth Leaders Assembly Endorses Governor Siminalayi Fubara for Second Term in Office
item.description: My people, the Rivers Youth Leaders Assembly don come out boldly to endorse Governor Siminalayi Fubara for second term in office till 2031. According to the Speaker of the Assembly, Prince Tonye Jemin...
